name: Comarca of Las Merindades in gravel
name_original: Comarca de Las Merindades en gravel
description:
To the north of the province of Burgos, on the Cantabrian mountain range, is the region of Las Merindades.

Glacial valleys, limestone mountains pierced by extensive and mysterious caves, flora, fauna, history and culture await you on this essential Route.

With almost 85 kilometers and 1,400 meters of cumulative elevation gain, you have a gravel journey ahead of you that will take you to discover a magical area that you will surely fall in love with.

Except for some short sections, the route is technically simple. The difficulties are more in the unevenness to overcome and in possible storms that may arise during the day.

The Route begins and ends at the Pedrosa de Valdeporres railway station, which you can easily reach by train with your bicycle.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Merindad De Valdeporres offers diverse terrain within the Merindades region of Burgos province. The landscape is characterized by extensive forests, including ancient oak and beech, alongside river valleys carved by the Nela and Engaña. Dramatic rock formations, such as the natural arch of Puentedey, add to the geological interest. This area provides a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces, suitable for various gravel biking challenges. Best gravel bike trails around Merindad De Valdeporres The most popular gravel bike trail is Comarca of Las Merindades in gravel, a 51.8 miles (83.3 km) trail that takes 8 hours 4 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation changes through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Puentedey – Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey loop from Villarcayo de Merindad de Castilla la Vieja, a moderate 33.0 miles (53.2 km) path. This route explores the area around the iconic natural rock arch and river valleys. Local gravel bikers also love the Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey – Puentedey loop from Pedrosa, a 25.3 miles (40.8 km) trail leading through the dramatic rock formations and rural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 23 minutes. Gravel biking around Merindad De Valdeporres is defined by extensive forests, river valleys, and dramatic rock formations, including sections of the Camino Natural Santander-Mediterráneo. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Merindad De Valdeporres are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 2.3 stars from 3 reviews. More than 60 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Merindad De Valdeporres's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: Based on komoot data, there are over 7 gravel bike trails in Merindad De Valdeporres, catering to various skill levels from easy to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: The region offers a diverse mix of terrain, from extensive forests and river valleys to dramatic rock formations. You'll find both paved and unpaved surfaces, including sections of the historic Camino Natural Santander-Mediterráneo, providing varied challenges for gravel bikers.

Q: Are there any easy gravel bike routes suitable for beginners in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for beginners. An easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40003867" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey – Puentedey loop from Villarcayo de Merindad de Castilla la Vieja&lt;/a&gt;, which is 23.9 km long with moderate elevation changes, perfect for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions I can see while gravel biking in the area?
A: Merindad De Valdeporres is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle past the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey&lt;/a&gt;, explore extensive forests, and follow river valleys like the Nela and Engaña. The region also features dramatic rock formations and impressive waterfalls.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible by gravel bike in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: Yes, the region is home to several beautiful waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7011001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CascadaLaMea&lt;/a&gt; is particularly impressive, especially after rain or snowmelt, and can be accessed via trails that are suitable for gravel bikes. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1926350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfalls on the Senda del Río Engaña&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular gravel bike routes in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38152178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puentedey – Natural Rock Arch of Puentedey loop from Villarcayo de Merindad de Castilla la Vieja&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route exploring the natural arch and river valleys.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: The region is generally pleasant for gravel biking during spring, summer, and early autumn. Spring and autumn offer milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes, while summer provides longer daylight hours. During periods of heavy rain or snowmelt, some trails, especially near waterfalls, might be more challenging.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 2.3 stars from 3 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural features like the Puentedey arch, and the tranquil rural environment that allows for an immersive outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites to explore along the gravel routes?
A: Beyond its natural beauty, Merindad De Valdeporres offers historical points of interest. You can discover megalithic monuments, such as the dolmen of Busnela, and other ancient architectural sites, adding a cultural dimension to your rides. The village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5455779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puentedey&lt;/a&gt; itself, built upon the natural rock arch, is a significant landmark.

Q: Is there a section of the Camino Natural Santander-Mediterráneo suitable for gravel biking in this region?
A: Yes, a notable section of the Camino Natural Santander-Mediterráneo, specifically around the Tunnel de la Engaña, has been converted into a Greenway (Vía Verde). This approximately 6-kilometer stretch offers an excellent, relatively flat path ideal for gravel bikes, connecting the tunnel to the old Pedrosa station.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced gravel bikers?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e833035982/comarca-of-las-merindades-in-gravel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comarca of Las Merindades in gravel&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation changes and varied terrain over 83.3 km, requiring good fitness. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38274293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trespaderne – Oña Historic Town Center loop from Villarcayo de Merindad de Castilla la Vieja&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the options for parking near gravel bike trails in Merindad De Valdeporres?
A: While specific parking areas are not detailed for every trail, the rural nature of Merindad De Valdeporres generally offers opportunities for parking in or near villages that serve as common starting points for routes. It's advisable to check local village parking regulations or designated areas upon arrival.
